evals.name: no-technical-references
evals.prompt: Generate journey J01 for a new collaborator signing up and getting their first match. The project has feature specs at docs/specs/features/ covering feature-onboarding, profiles, feature-matching, and feature-collab.
evals.expected_output: Journey doc in user language with zero file names, DB columns, CSS selectors, component names, or code paths in scenario steps. AC IDs (like @AUTH-01) are the only technical elements.

assertions.text: No .tsx or .ts file references in scenario steps
No database column references (profiles., _at, _id patterns) in scenario steps
No CSS selectors or data attributes in scenario steps
No code paths or route patterns in scenario steps
AC IDs present as @XX-NN tags above scenarios
Has ## Behavior Specification section with Gherkin keywords
Uses Given/When/Then as list items

evals.name: three-layer-analysis
evals.prompt: Generate journey J01 for the new collaborator first match flow. Include all three layers of analysis: what IS (from specs), what SHOULD BE (implementation status), and what's MISSING (product gaps and journey reasoning).
evals.expected_output: Journey with [LIVE]/[SPEC]/[PLANNED] status tags on scenarios, plus a Journey Analysis section with logical issues, missing transitions, and product gaps tables.

assertions.text: Contains [LIVE] or [SPEC] or [PLANNED] status tags
Has Journey Analysis section
Has Logical Issues table or subsection
Has Missing Transitions table or subsection
Has Product Gaps table or subsection

evals.name: absolute-detail-not-abstract
evals.prompt: Generate journey J01 for signup to first match. Make sure scenarios are absolute - every step describes what the user sees, decides, and what happens.
evals.expected_output: Scenarios with specific UI detail (button labels, screen descriptions, visual indicators, timing) not abstract summaries like 'I see a loading state' or 'I get a match'.

assertions.text: Scenarios mention specific button labels in quotes
Scenarios describe what user sees on screen (visual detail)
Scenarios include alternative outcomes (But/if clauses)
assertions.pattern: - But|- And if|one of two things|either

assertions.text: No generic steps like 'I get a match' without detail

evals.name: journey-reasoning-finds-issues
evals.prompt: Generate journey J01 and run Layer 3 analysis. The journey crosses feature-onboarding, profiles, feature-matching, and feature-collab. Find logical contradictions, dead ends, wrong placement, missing transitions, and redundancies across the end-to-end flow.
evals.expected_output: Layer 3 analysis identifies at least 3 specific findings: contradictions between specs, missing transitions between features, dead-end states, or sequencing problems. Each finding names where in the journey, what's wrong, and why it matters.

assertions.text: Identifies at least 3 Layer 3 findings
Each finding specifies WHERE in the journey
Each finding explains WHY it matters for the user
Findings are user-facing problems, not technical debt

evals.name: auto-mode-coverage
evals.prompt: Run /journey-sync --auto on this project. Process all feature specs, identify all personas, generate all journeys until every persona has at least one journey and every user-facing feature spec is referenced. Don't stop until complete.
evals.expected_output: Multiple journey docs generated covering all personas. JOURNEY_INDEX.md written with coverage stats. Auto mode summary showing personas, journeys, AC coverage percentage, and Layer 3 findings count.

assertions.text: JOURNEY_INDEX.md exists with persona table
Multiple journey files created (at least 3)
Every persona has at least 1 journey
Coverage report shows AC percentage
Auto mode summary present

evals.name: pm-readable-no-code-knowledge-needed
evals.prompt: Generate journey J01. A product manager who has never seen the codebase should be able to read it and understand exactly what a new user experiences when signing up.
evals.expected_output: The journey reads like a product document. No engineering jargon. A PM can describe the experience back after reading it without asking any technical questions.

assertions.text: No mention of Supabase, Firebase, Playwright, React, or any framework
No mention of API, endpoint, RPC, or backend
No mention of specific auth implementation details
Uses plain English for system behavior

evals.name: bdd-scope-correct
evals.prompt: Generate journey J01. Include alternative paths but only those that are user behavior paths. Do NOT include emotional narration or infrastructure failure scenarios.
evals.expected_output: Alternative paths describe user choices (skip tour, cancel auth, no match found) but NOT emotional states or infrastructure failures.

assertions.text: No emotional narration in scenarios
No infrastructure failure scenarios
Has user-choice alternative paths
